message: Jake
Tim
Hey, Detective.
I’m glad, I thought I was
going to die.
I’m still alive, somehow.
Hey, Detective. Are you back again?
Look
Talk
Listen
Think
Move
System
Surroundings
About Ariana.
About Meryl.
About Kristy.
About　the　future.
About.
About Meryl.

Save
Exit the game.
He seems to have already finished preparing to leave this room. There’s hardly anything here that tells of his having lived in this room.
It was just a short time,
the room where he lived with Cindy. It will also become an unforgettable memory for me.
It must not have been easy for a young man like him to live in New York. There are probably memories he can’t let go of.
I wonder if he’s calmed down a bit; his expression maintains a tranquility that could also be interpreted as resignation.
From his emaciated body, the flesh has dropped even further, leaving almost just bones.
Even though a new life awaits him, I wonder if he’ll be okay looking so listless. I can’t help but worry.
Detective.
Humans are mysterious, aren't they?
Huh?
I thought I absolutely couldn't go on living if Ariana died.
But I'm still this full of life.
People can endure any sorrow. Besides, if Tim dies, Ariana will be sad.
No, that's not right. I'm sure.
You're late! I thought you would come sooner!
He's getting angry.
By the way, you quit your part-time job, huh?
Yeah.
I'm not in a state to work right now.
I have something I want to discuss with the detective.
I've been waiting.
I thought this town was exciting, and that dreams could be easily caught.
But reality wasn't that sweet.
Actually, a woman came to see Ariana once.
Do you know her?
No, I've never seen her.
But it felt like Ariana knew her.
At first, I thought it might be Jennifer.
You know, those two get along well, right? But it was different.
Was it Meryl?
Meryl?
Actually, she was Ariana's mother. She came here the other day. It was pitiful.
I see, Meryl was here.
Yeah.
Besides Meryl, another woman named Kristy Dern came, and she visited before the mother drama.
Wait a minute. Did you just say Kristy Dern?
Yeah! Do you know her, detective? She was talking to Ariana looking all troubled.
I don't know the content.
I'll remember her fondly. It's still tough now, but someday for sure.
I was surprised when Meryl came, but looking closely, she looks like Ariana.
Yeah.
And you know, he said he wanted to take revenge on the guy who killed Ariana while looking really sad, so I stopped him.
If Ariana knew that Meryl was her mom, she'd probably be really happy. She was a fan of hers, you know.
Is that so? Did you tell Meryl about that?
Yeah, of course. That made her smile really happily.
She's laughing while shedding tears, isn't that strange?
Saying that,
she was smiling with tears streaming down her face.
I wish they could have at least met as mother and daughter.
Do you remember what that woman, the one who called herself Kristy, was like?
Hmm,
her hair was about shoulder-length, and she had a lady-like vibe.
I wonder what those two were talking about?
Something about Guy, right?
I thought it was something like that. But Ariana looked really annoyed and angry.
Could that woman be the criminal?
I'm leaving this town to help on my relative's farm.
I see, good luck.
Of course. That relative has always been kind to me, and we've been talking about this for a while.
I'm glad I could talk to the detective. I was leaving today. I'm happy I got to talk to you one last time.
I see, you're leaving already.
Take care and do your best.
Yeah, you take care too, detective.
Do you think I fit in on a farm?
I'm a bit anxious about it.
More than anything, leaving New York is probably the hardest part. If I go to a new place, maybe I'll see more opportunities.
I wonder if I'll regret giving up my dreams so easily? But I can't say to stick around and try hard here.
I wonder what will happen from here on out. An enormous setback, a lover that I can never forget.
Reality is a cruel thing.
I did terrible things to Meryl.
I at least wanted to let Cindy see her, even just once, while she was still alive.
No matter how much I regret it, if I had just introduced the two of them a bit earlier, this tragedy might not have happened.
Meryl.
I had no idea she had been visiting her. Did she want to touch on her daughter's life even a little while she was alive?
To Meryl, she was a daughter who shouldn't have existed in the first place, so it seems like she could have given up.
In theory, that's true.
Kristy seems to be falling deeper into madness than ever. Is it really okay to leave her like this?
If her condition gets any worse, I'll have to find a way to get Günter involved. Maybe lock her up somewhere.
Kristy came to see Cindy? Why would she do such a thing? First of all, how did she find out about this place?
No way, could it be that Kristy killed Cindy?
That's ridiculous.
I can't believe such a thing.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
